question: Given the following code fragment, what is its Big O running time?

answers.feedback: No. $O(n)$ would be correct if there were only one loop.
Correct! The outer loop runs $n$ times and the inner loop also runs $n$ times for each iteration of the outer loop, resulting in $n \times n = n^2$ total operations.
No. Logarithmic time usually involves dividing the input size in each step, such as in binary search.
No. $O(n^3)$ would require three nested loops iterating up to $n$.
